stimulus: Individual giraffes and many large modern antelopes tend to remain within stable home ranges over the course of their lives, which had led some paleontologists to speculate that Sivatherium giganteum, an extinct giraffid of the Asian plains, might have behaved the same way. Sivatherium teeth grew in distinct enamel layers, incorporating dietary minerals as they formed, so each layer records the chemical fingerprint of the food the animal consumed during a particular interval of its life. By comparing the strontium isotope ratios across these layers to the ratios characteristic of various regional bedrocks, Mehmet Gunay and colleagues showed that several Sivatherium individuals had moved across hundreds of kilometers during their lifetimes, contrary to the assumption of a fixed range.
stem: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined statement in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It introduces a trait shared by certain animals in order to contextualize a hypothesis about the origin of that trait that is later advanced in the text.
It describes a behavioral pattern in modern animals in order to explain why researchers raised a possibility about Sivatherium that was not borne out by the data described later in the text.
It illustrates a pattern of behavior in order to introduce a theory about exceptions to that pattern that is weakened by findings described later in the text.
It describes a similarity in the behavior of certain animals in order to show why a method later described in the text could not reveal whether Sivatherium also showed that behavior.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The underlined sentence reports that giraffes and many large antelopes stay within stable home ranges, which is presented as the basis on which some paleontologists speculated that Sivatherium would have done likewise. The rest of the text then uses Gunay and colleagues' isotopic evidence to show that this expectation did not hold for Sivatherium. The underlined material thus explains why the hypothesis was raised before being undermined by later evidence.

Choice A is incorrect because no hypothesis about the origin of the trait is advanced. Choice C is incorrect because the text does not introduce a theory of exceptions to home-range behavior. Choice D is incorrect because the isotopic method clearly succeeds in revealing Sivatherium's movement patterns.
